steps.description: Acid-reflux exfoliated GS (50 mg) in 5 mol/L HNO3 (40 mL) at 100 °C for 24 h with continuous stirring to purify and reduce flake size
Rinse mixture with DI water and ethanol to remove acid and bring pH to neutral

human_recipe.notes: PGS is the intermediate consumed in hydrothermal GQD synthesis (GQD1-4).

steps.description: Suspend PGS in a mixture of 5 mol/L H3PO4 (20 mL) and ethylenediamine (30 mL)
Probe-sonicate the suspension for 2 h
Transfer to 100 mL Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave and heat at 200 °C for 4 h
Cool autoclave to room temperature (30 °C)
Wash brown colloidal supernatant with ethanol and DI water to remove residual ethylenediamine
Separate larger graphene nanoparticles by vacuum filtration through 0.22 µm filter paper; collect filtrate
Dialyse collected filtrate over DI water using dialysis membrane-50 for 24 h to obtain GQD1

human_recipe.notes: GQD1 = 4 h hydrothermal duration. Ethylenediamine acts as surface passivation agent reducing defects. Product is a clear brown solution after dialysis.

steps.description: Suspend the previously obtainedPGS in a mixture of 5 mol/L H3PO4 (20 mL) and ethylenediamine (30 mL)
Probe-sonicate the suspension for 2 h
Transfer to 100 mL Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave and heat at 200 °C for 8 h
Cool autoclave to room temperature (30 °C)
Wash brown colloidal supernatant with ethanol and DI water to remove residual ethylenediamine
Separate larger graphene nanoparticles by vacuum filtration through 0.22 µm filter paper; collect filtrate
Dialyse collected filtrate over DI water using dialysis membrane-50 for 24 h to obtain GQD2

human_recipe.notes: GQD2 = 8 h hydrothermal duration, identified as optimal. Size ca. 6–10 nm, strong blue fluorescence, ID/IG = 0.78, C/O = 2. No N or P doping detected by EDX.

steps.description: Suspend PGS in a mixture of 5 mol/L H3PO4 (20 mL) and ethylenediamine (30 mL)
Probe-sonicate the suspension for 2 h
Transfer to 100 mL Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave and heat at 200 °C for 12 h
Cool autoclave to room temperature (30 °C)
Wash brown colloidal supernatant with ethanol and DI water to remove residual ethylenediamine
Separate larger graphene nanoparticles by vacuum filtration through 0.22 µm filter paper; collect filtrate
Dialyse collected filtrate over DI water using dialysis membrane-50 for 24 h to obtain GQD3

human_recipe.notes: GQD3 = 12 h hydrothermal duration. Yielded nonuniform and larger particles compared to GQD2 (8 h optimum).

steps.description: Suspend PGS in DI water only (no H3PO4, no ethylenediamine)
Transfer to 100 mL Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave and heat at 200 °C for 8 h
Cool autoclave to room temperature (30 °C)
Separate larger graphene nanoparticles by vacuum filtration through 0.22 µm filter paper; collect filtrate
Dialyse filtrate over DI water for 24 h to obtain GQD4

human_recipe.notes: Control sample — no surface passivation (no ethylenediamine) and no H3PO4. Supercritical water acts as reducing agent. Lower fluorescence and higher defects than GQD2 (ID/IG = 0.94). Scanty GQD distribution on GS surface by FESEM.

steps.description: Set up 2-electrode system with both anode and cathode as graphite electrodes, facing parallel, separated by 2 cm, immersed in 0.1 mol/L NaOH
Apply 10 V DC for ~40 min; solution transforms from colourless to light brown to dark brown indicating GQD formation
Wash solution thoroughly with DI water and ethanol to obtain neutral pH
Remove larger graphite particles by vacuum filtration through 0.22 µm filter paper; collect filtrate
Dialyse filtrate over DI water for 24 h to obtain GQD5; solvent can then be evaporated to obtain powder redispersible in DI water or other solvents

human_recipe.notes: Electrochemical process introduces more oxygen groups and sp3 defects vs hydrothermal. GQD5 shows higher ID/IG than GQD4, nonuniform and bigger particles. Multiple emission peaks (375, 394, 414 nm). Fluorescence order: GQD2 > GQD5 > GQD4 > PGS.
